columns.name: AdditionalFields
columns.description: Additional information, represented using key/value pairs provided by the source which do not map to ASim.

columns.name: _BilledSize
columns.description: The record size in bytes

columns.name: DnsFlags
columns.description: The DNS request flags, as provided by the reporting device. The structure of the DNS flags information may vary between different reporting devices.

columns.name: DnsFlagsAuthenticated
columns.description: The DNS authenticated answer flag, which is related to DNSSEC, indicates in a response that all data included in the answer and authority sections of the response have been verified by the server according to the policies of that server. see RFC 3655 Section 6.1 for more information.

columns.name: DnsFlagsAuthoritative
columns.description: The DNS authoritative answer flag indicates whether the response from the server was authoritative.

columns.name: DnsFlagsCheckingDisabled
columns.description: The DNS CD flag, which is related to DNSSEC, indicates in a query that non-verified data is acceptable to the system sending the query.

columns.name: DnsFlagsRecursionAvailable
columns.description: The DNS RA flag indicates in a response that that server supports recursive queries.

columns.name: DnsFlagsRecursionDesired
columns.description: The DNS recursion desired flag indicates in a request that that client would like the server to use recursive queries.

columns.name: DnsFlagsTruncated
columns.description: The DNS TC flag indicates that a response was truncates as it exceeded the maximum response size.

columns.name: DnsFlagsZ
columns.description: The DNS Z flag is a deprecated DNS flag, which might be reported by older DNS systems.

columns.name: DnsNetworkDuration
columns.description: The amount of time, in milliseconds, for the completion of DNS request.

columns.name: DnsQuery
columns.description: The domain that needs to be resolved.

columns.name: DnsQueryClass
columns.description: The DNS class ID as defined by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA).

columns.name: DnsQueryClassName
columns.description: The DNS class name as defined by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA).

columns.name: DnsQueryType
columns.description: The DNS resource record type codes as defined by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA).

columns.name: DnsQueryTypeName
columns.description: The DNS resource record type name as defined by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA).

columns.name: DnsResponseCode
columns.description: The DNS numerical response code as defined by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA).

columns.name: DnsResponseIpCity
columns.description: The city associated with the response IP address.

columns.name: DnsResponseIpCountry
columns.description: The country associated with the response IP address.

columns.name: DnsResponseIpLatitude
columns.description: The Latitude of the geographical coordinate associated with the response IP address.

columns.name: DnsResponseIpLongitude
columns.description: The longitude of the geographical coordinate associated with the response IP address.

columns.name: DnsResponseIpRegion
columns.description: The region, or state, within a country, associated with the source IP address.

columns.name: DnsResponseName
columns.description: The content of the response, as included in the record. The structure of the DNS response data may vary between different reporting devices.

columns.name: DnsSessionId
columns.description: The DNS session identifier as reported by the reporting device.

columns.name: Dst
columns.description: A unique identifier of the server that received the DNS request.

columns.name: DstDescription
columns.description: A descriptive text associated with the destination.

columns.name: DstDeviceType
columns.description: The type of the destination device.

columns.name: DstDomain
columns.description: The domain of the destination device.

columns.name: DstDomainType
columns.description: The type of DstDomain.

columns.name: DstDvcId
columns.description: The ID of the destination device.

columns.name: DstDvcIdType
columns.description: The type of DstDvcId.

columns.name: DstDvcScope
columns.description: The cloud platform scope the destination device belongs to. DvcScope maps to a subscription on Azure and to an account on AWS.

columns.name: DstDvcScopeId
columns.description: The cloud platform scope ID the destination device belongs to. DvcScopeId map to a subscription ID on Azure and to an account ID on AWS.

columns.name: DstFQDN
columns.description: The destination device hostname, including domain information when available.

columns.name: DstGeoCity
columns.description: The city associated with the destination IP address.

columns.name: DstGeoCountry
columns.description: The country associated with the destination IP address.

columns.name: DstGeoLatitude
columns.description: The latitude of the geographical coordinate associated with the destination IP address.

columns.name: DstGeoLongitude
columns.description: The longitude of the geographical coordinate associated with the destination IP address.

columns.name: DstGeoRegion
columns.description: The region, or state, within a country, associated with the destination IP address.

columns.name: DstHostname
columns.description: The destination device hostname, excluding domain information.

columns.name: DstIpAddr
columns.description: The IP address of the server receiving the DNS request. For a regular DNS request, this value would typically be the reporting device, and in most cases set to 127.0.0.1.

columns.name: DstOriginalRiskLevel
columns.description: The risk level associated with the destination device as reported by the reporting device.

columns.name: DstPortNumber
columns.description: Destination Port number.

columns.name: DstRiskLevel
columns.description: The risk level associated with the destination device.

columns.name: Dvc
columns.description: A unique identifier of the device reporting the event. The identifier can be either an IP Address, A hostname, or a device ID.

columns.name: DvcAction
columns.description: The action taken by the the reporting device on the request, such as blocking it.

columns.name: DvcDescription
columns.description: A descriptive text associated with the device. For example: Primary Domain Controller.

columns.name: DvcDomain
columns.description: The domain of the device reporting the event.

columns.name: DvcDomainType
columns.description: The type of DvcDomain. Possible values include "Windows" and "FQDN".

columns.name: DvcFQDN
columns.description: The fully qualified hostname, including domain information, of the device reporting the event.

columns.name: DvcHostname
columns.description: The hostname of the device reporting the event.

columns.name: DvcId
columns.description: The unique ID of the device reporting the event.

columns.name: DvcIdType
columns.description: The type of DvcId.

columns.name: DvcInterface
columns.description: The network interface on which data was captured. This field is typically relevant to network related activity which is captured by an intermediate or tap device.

columns.name: DvcIpAddr
columns.description: The IP Address of the device reporting the event.

columns.name: DvcMacAddr
columns.description: The MAC address of the device reporting the event.

columns.name: DvcOriginalAction
columns.description: The original DvcAction as provided by the reporting device.

columns.name: DvcOs
columns.description: The operating system running on the device reporting the event.

columns.name: DvcOsVersion
columns.description: The version of the operating system on the device reporting the event.

columns.name: DvcScope
columns.description: The cloud platform scope the device belongs to. DvcScope map to a subscription ID on Azure and to an account ID on AWS.

columns.name: DvcScopeId
columns.description: The cloud platform scope ID the device belongs to. DvcScopeId map to a subscription ID on Azure and to an account ID on AWS.

columns.name: DvcZone
columns.description: The network segment of the device reporting the event.

columns.name: EventCount
columns.description: The number of events described by the record. This value is used when the source supports aggregation, and a single record may represent multiple events.

columns.name: EventEndTime
columns.description: The time at which the event ended. If the source supports aggregation and the record represents multiple events, the time that the last event was generated. If not provided by the source record, this field aliases the TimeGenerated field.

columns.name: EventMessage
columns.description: A general message or description.

columns.name: EventOriginalSeverity
columns.description: The original severity as provided by the reporting device. This value is used to derive EventSeverity.

columns.name: EventOriginalType
columns.description: The original event type or ID, for example, the original Windows event ID.

columns.name: EventOriginalUid
columns.description: A unique ID of the original record.

columns.name: EventOwner
columns.description: The owner of the event, which is usually the department or subsidiary in which it was generated.

columns.name: EventProduct
columns.description: The product generating the event.

columns.name: EventProductVersion
columns.description: The version of the product generating the event.

columns.name: EventReportUrl
columns.description: A URL of a resource that provides additional information about the event.

columns.name: EventResult
columns.description: The outcome of the event, represented by one of the following values: Success, Partial, Failure, NA (Not Applicable). The value may not be provided directly by the sources, in which case it is derived from other event fields, for example, the EventResultDetails field.

columns.name: EventResultDetails
columns.description: The DNS response code as defined by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA).

columns.name: EventSchemaVersion
columns.description: The version of the schema.

columns.name: EventSeverity
columns.description: The severity of the event. Valid values are: Informational, Low, Medium, or High.

columns.name: EventStartTime
columns.description: The time at which the event started. If the source supports aggregation and the record represents multiple events, the time that the first event was generated. If not provided by the source record, this field aliases the TimeGenerated field.

columns.name: EventSubType
columns.description: Either request or response.

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: Indicates the operation reported by the record. For DNS activity events, this value is the DNS opcode as defined by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA).

columns.name: EventVendor
columns.description: The vendor of the product generating the event.

columns.name: _IsBillable
columns.description: Specifies whether ingesting the data is billable. When _IsBillable isfalseingestion isn't billed to your Azure account

columns.name: NetworkProtocol
columns.description: The transport protocol used by the network resolution event. The value can be UDP or TCP.

columns.name: NetworkProtocolVersion
columns.description: The version of the network protocol. Typically used to differentiate between IPv4 and Ipv6.

columns.name: _ResourceId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the resource that the record is associated with

columns.name: RuleName
columns.description: The name or ID of the rule by associated with the inspection results.

columns.name: RuleNumber
columns.description: The number of the rule associated with the inspection results.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example,OpsManagerfor Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager,Linuxfor all Linux agents, orAzurefor Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: Src
columns.description: A unique identifier of the source device.

columns.name: SrcDescription
columns.description: The number of the rule associated with the inspection results.

columns.name: SrcDeviceType
columns.description: The type of the source device.

columns.name: SrcDomain
columns.description: The domain of the source device.

columns.name: SrcDomainType
columns.description: The type of SrcDomain.

columns.name: SrcDvcId
columns.description: The ID of the source device.

columns.name: SrcDvcIdType
columns.description: The type of SrcDvcId.

columns.name: SrcDvcScope
columns.description: The cloud platform scope the source device belongs to. DvcScope maps to a subscription on Azure and to an account on AWS.

columns.name: SrcDvcScopeId
columns.description: The cloud platform scope ID the source device belongs to. DvcScopeId map to a subscription ID on Azure and to an account ID on AWS.

columns.name: SrcFQDN
columns.description: The source device hostname, including domain information.

columns.name: SrcGeoCity
columns.description: The city associated with the source IP address.

columns.name: SrcGeoCountry
columns.description: The country associated with the source IP address.

columns.name: SrcGeoLatitude
columns.description: The latitude of the geographical coordinate associated with the source IP address.

columns.name: SrcGeoLongitude
columns.description: The longitude of the geographical coordinate associated with the source IP address.

columns.name: SrcGeoRegion
columns.description: The region, or state, within a country, associated with the source IP address.

columns.name: SrcHostname
columns.description: The source device hostname, excluding domain information.

columns.name: SrcIpAddr
columns.description: The IP address of the client sending the DNS request. For a recursive DNS request, this value would typically be the reporting device, and in most cases, set to 127.0.0.1.

columns.name: SrcOriginalRiskLevel
columns.description: The risk level associated with the source device as reported by the reporting device.

columns.name: SrcOriginalUserType
columns.description: The original source user type, as provided by the source.

columns.name: SrcPortNumber
columns.description: Source port of the DNS query.

columns.name: SrcProcessGuid
columns.description: A generated unique identifier (GUID) of the process that initiated the DNS request.

columns.name: SrcProcessId
columns.description: The process ID (PID) of the process that initiated the DNS request.

columns.name: SrcProcessName
columns.description: The name of the process that initiated the DNS request.

columns.name: SrcRiskLevel
columns.description: The risk level associated with the source device.

columns.name: SrcUserId
columns.description: A machine-readable, alphanumeric, unique representation of the source user.

columns.name: SrcUserIdType
columns.description: The type of the ID stored in the SrcUserId field.

columns.name: SrcUsername
columns.description: The Source username, including domain information when available.

columns.name: SrcUsernameType
columns.description: The type of the username stored in the SrcUsername field.

columns.name: SrcUserScope
columns.description: The scope, such as Azure AD tenant, in which SrcUserId and SrcUsername are defined.

columns.name: SrcUserScopeId
columns.description: The ID of the scope, such as Azure AD tenant, in which SrcUserId and SrcUsername are defined.

columns.name: SrcUserSessionId
columns.description: The unique ID of the sign-in session of the source user.

columns.name: SrcUserType
columns.description: The type of the source user.

columns.name: _SubscriptionId
columns.description: A unique identifier for the subscription that the record is associated with

columns.name: TenantId
columns.description: The Log Analytics workspace ID

columns.name: ThreatCategory
columns.description: If a DNS event source also provides DNS security, it may also evaluate the DNS event. For example, it can search for the IP address or domain in a threat intelligence database, and assign the domain or IP address with a Threat Category.

columns.name: ThreatConfidence
columns.description: The confidence level of the threat identified, normalized to a value between 0 and a 100.

columns.name: ThreatField
columns.description: The field for which a threat was identified. The value is either SrcIpAddr, DstIpAddr, Domain, or DnsResponseName.

columns.name: ThreatFirstReportedTime
columns.description: The first time the IP address or domain were identified as a threat.

columns.name: ThreatFirstReportedTime_d
columns.description: The first time the IP address or domain were identified as a threat.

columns.name: ThreatId
columns.description: The ID of the threat or malware identified in the web session.

columns.name: ThreatIpAddr
columns.description: An IP address for which a threat was identified. The field ThreatField contains the name of the field ThreatIpAddr represents. If a threat is identified in the Domain field, this field should be empty.

columns.name: ThreatIsActive
columns.description: True ID the threat identified is considered an active threat.

columns.name: ThreatLastReportedTime
columns.description: The last time the IP address or domain were identified as a threat.

columns.name: ThreatLastReportedTime_d
columns.description: The last time the IP address or domain were identified as a threat.

columns.name: ThreatName
columns.description: The name of the threat identified, as reported by the reporting device.

columns.name: ThreatOriginalConfidence
columns.description: The original confidence level of the threat identified, as reported by the reporting device.

columns.name: ThreatOriginalRiskLevel
columns.description: The original risk level associated with the threat identified, as reported by the reporting device.

columns.name: ThreatOriginalRiskLevel_s
columns.description: The risk level associated with the threat identified, normalized to a value between 0 and a 100.

columns.name: ThreatRiskLevel
columns.description: The risk level associated with the threat identified, normalized to a value between 0 and a 100.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) reflecting the time in which the event was generated.

columns.name: TransactionIdHex
columns.description: The DNS unique hex transaction ID.

columns.name: Type
columns.description: The name of the table

columns.name: UrlCategory
columns.description: A DNS event source may also look up the category of the requested Domains.
